statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Steve Kerr, Doc Rivers join 'political interference' letter

articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: Ejiofor leads Senior Night comeback as No. 18 St. John's tops Georgetown 72-69 at MSG
article.description: — Zuby Ejiofor scored 23 points, Joson Sanon added 15 off the bench — all after halftime — and No. 18 St. John's rallied from 12 down in the second half to beat skidding Georgetown 72-69 on Tuesday night.
article.linkText: Ejiofor leads Senior Night comeback as No. 18 St. John's tops Georgetown 72-69 at MSG

categories.description: Georgetown Hoyas @ St. John's Red Storm

event.description: Georgetown Hoyas @ St. John's Red Storm

keywords: Georgetown Hoyas @ St. John's Red Storm
article.story: NEW YORK -- — Zuby Ejiofor scored 23 points, Joson Sanon added 15 off the bench — all after halftime — and No. 18 St. John's rallied from 12 down in the second half to beat skidding Georgetown 72-69 on Tuesday night. With a gritty comeback on Senior Night at Madison Square Garden, the Red Storm (24-6, 17-2 Big East) tied No. 4 UConn atop the conference standings with one regular-season game remaining. A victory Friday night at Hudson River rival Seton Hall would give the Johnnies at least a share of their second consecutive Big East regular-season championship following a 32-year drought. The only time they’ve won back-to-back league crowns was 1985 and ’86. Dylan Darling had 12 points for St. John's, which has won 15 of 16 overall and 11 straight meetings with Georgetown — the longest streak for either school in a series that dates to 1909. Kayvaun Mulready scored a career-high 20 points off the bench for the streaky Hoyas (13-17, 5-14), last in the Big East. They've lost a season-worst seven in a row following a four-game winning streak. Jeremiah Williams shook off an injury to his lower right leg and added 17 points. Malik Mack had 15. After honoring Ejiofor and four other seniors in a pregame ceremony, the Red Storm came out flat. They trailed 48-36 with 13:30 remaining before finally starting to find their rhythm when Ian Jackson converted a three-point play. Darling’s steal and fast-break layup put St. John’s ahead 59-57 with 6:43 left, its first lead since 15-14 with 12:16 to go in the first half. A jumper by Sanon capped an 8-0 run, but Georgetown wouldn’t go away even after falling behind by five with 1:30 to play. Williams could have tied it by finishing a three-point play with 16.6 seconds left, but he missed the free throw. Bryce Hopkins grabbed the rebound and sank two foul shots to make it 72-69, and Caleb Williams missed a 3-pointer for the Hoyas just before the buzzer. Up next Georgetown coach Ed Cooley faces his former team again when the Hoyas host Providence in their regular-season finale Saturday night. St. John’s stormed back from 15 down in the second half to defeat Seton Hall 65-60 at MSG on Jan. 20. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season.
